text: Ice Maker Assembly Installation Guide Unplug the refrigerator from the wall outlet or turn off the circuit breaker before beginning any repair. Remove all food and ice from the freezer compartment. Locate the ice maker on the upper left side of the freezer interior.

Unplug the refrigerator from the wall outlet or turn off the circuit breaker before beginning any repair. Remove all food and ice from the freezer compartment. Locate the ice maker on the upper left side of the freezer interior. Remove the two mounting screws (Phillips #2) that secure the ice maker to the freezer wall. Hold the ice maker with one hand while removing the second screw.

Pull the ice maker forward slightly, then disconnect the wiring harness by squeezing the locking tab and pulling the connector straight out. Disconnect the water fill tube by pulling it straight off the inlet fitting on the old ice maker. Have a small towel ready to catch any residual water. Connect the wiring harness to the new ice maker assembly until you hear a click, then attach the water fill tube to the inlet fitting.

Position the new ice maker against the freezer wall, aligning the mounting holes, and secure with the two screws removed earlier. Restore power to the refrigerator. The ice maker will cycle within 24 hours once the freezer reaches 15°F (−9°C) or below.

Dishwasher Lower Spray Arm Replacement Open the dishwasher door and remove the bottom rack completely to access the lower spray arm. Grasp the spray arm and pull it straight up to unsnap it from the center wash manifold. On some models you may need to turn it counterclockwise ¼ turn first.

Open the dishwasher door and remove the bottom rack completely to access the lower spray arm. Grasp the spray arm and pull it straight up to unsnap it from the center wash manifold. On some models you may need to turn it counterclockwise ¼ turn first. Inspect the wash manifold and filter area for debris and clean if necessary before installing the new spray arm.

Align the new spray arm over the center post and press down firmly until it clicks into place. Spin it by hand to confirm it rotates freely. Replace the bottom rack and run a short wash cycle to verify proper water distribution.

Refrigerator Water Filter Replacement Locate the water filter housing in the upper-right interior of the refrigerator compartment. Turn the old filter counterclockwise ¼ turn and pull it straight out. Some water may drip — place a towel underneath.

Locate the water filter housing in the upper-right interior of the refrigerator compartment. Turn the old filter counterclockwise ¼ turn and pull it straight out. Some water may drip — place a towel underneath. Remove the protective cap from the new filter and insert it into the housing.
Push the filter in and turn it clockwise ¼ turn until it locks. Run 4 gallons (about 3 minutes) of water through the dispenser to flush the new filter before drinking.

Dishwasher Door Latch Assembly Replacement Unplug the dishwasher or turn off the circuit breaker. Open the dishwasher door fully. Remove the screws along the inner door panel perimeter (typically Torx T20). Keep track of all screws.

Unplug the dishwasher or turn off the circuit breaker. Open the dishwasher door fully. Remove the screws along the inner door panel perimeter (typically Torx T20). Keep track of all screws. Carefully separate the inner and outer door panels. The latch assembly is clipped to the top-center of the inner panel.

Disconnect the wiring connector from the door latch micro switch. Release the retaining clips and remove the old latch assembly. Snap the new latch assembly into position and reconnect the wiring connector.
Reassemble the door panels and reinstall all screws. Restore power and test the door latch.
ice maker not working

Check that the ice maker is switched ON. There is typically a wire arm or a power switch on the ice maker itself — make sure it is in the ON/down position. Verify the freezer temperature is at or below 15°F (−9°C). The ice maker will not cycle if the freezer is too warm. Adjust the temperature setting and wait 24 hours. Inspect the water supply line behind the refrigerator. Ensure the saddle valve or shut-off valve is fully open and the line is not kinked or frozen.

Replace the water filter if it has been more than 6 months since the last change. A clogged filter can restrict water flow to the ice maker. Check the ice maker fill tube (the small tube that delivers water into the ice tray) for ice blockage. If frozen, carefully thaw with a hair dryer on a low setting. If none of the above resolves the issue, the ice maker assembly itself may be faulty and require replacement. Part PS11752778 is a common replacement for GE models.
refrigerator not cooling

Check that the refrigerator is plugged in and the circuit breaker is not tripped. Verify the temperature controls are set correctly — refrigerator should be between 35°F and 38°F (1.7°C to 3.3°C). Check the condenser coils (usually behind or underneath the unit). Dusty coils prevent heat dissipation. Vacuum them clean.

Listen for the evaporator fan motor running. If the freezer is cold but the refrigerator is warm, the evaporator fan or damper control may be faulty. Inspect the door gaskets for tears or gaps that allow warm air to enter. Press a piece of paper against the gasket and close the door — if you can pull it out easily, the gasket needs replacing. If the compressor is not running, check the temperature control thermostat (part WR55X10025 for GE models). A faulty thermostat will prevent the compressor from starting.
dishwasher not cleaning dishes

Remove and inspect the lower spray arm (part W10195682 for Whirlpool models). Check that all spray holes are open and unobstructed — use a toothpick to clear any clogged holes. Remove and clean the dishwasher filter/screen at the bottom of the tub. A clogged filter is the most common cause of poor cleaning performance. Check water temperature — the dishwasher needs water at 120°F (49°C) for effective cleaning. Run the hot water at your kitchen sink until hot before starting the dishwasher.

Verify detergent is fresh (clumped or old detergent does not dissolve properly) and the dispenser door opens during the wash cycle. Ensure dishes are not blocking the spray arm rotation. Load dishes so spray can reach all surfaces and do not nest items together.
dishwasher not draining

Clean the dishwasher filter and the drain area at the bottom of the tub. A blocked filter is the most common cause of drainage problems. Check the drain hose (runs from the pump to the sink drain or garbage disposal) for kinks, clogs, or improper installation. The hose should have a high loop or air gap to prevent backflow. If connected to a garbage disposal, verify the knockout plug inside the disposal inlet has been removed.
Run the garbage disposal to clear any blockage in the shared drain line. If the drain pump motor is faulty, you will hear no pump sound during the drain cycle. The pump may require replacement.
